canonicalChoiceReason: User-designated keep slug. Published v2 action_card with primary responseRole, real source URLs, and guide images. Cleaner action-verb slug.
layerMismatchNote: These guides are in different layers (action vs scenario) and different guideTypes (action_card vs scenario_guide). Normally the consolidator would not merge cross-layer guides, but this consolidation was explicitly requested. The merged guide retains the canonical guide's layer (action) and guideType (action_card). Unique scenario-layer content (Ottawa Ankle Rules tip, ER vs urgent care decision guidance) has been folded into the merged steps and tips.

fieldDecisions.note: Action-verb phrasing 'Sprain or Suspected Fracture: Immobilize and Cool' is clearer for a panicked user than the generic 'Sprains and Suspected Fractures'.

fieldDecisions.source: Both guides contributed. Archive guide added the decision-making framing about when to call 911 vs wait for urgent care.

fieldDecisions.note: Canonical version is more actionable and specific.

fieldDecisions.source: Both. Archive version added 'arrange medical evaluation / X-ray if cannot bear weight'. Merged into canonical's stronger action phrasing.
Both are similar. Archive added 'do not attempt to walk the person out on an injured lower limb without immobilization'. Merged.

10 steps from canonical + 9 steps from archive. Deduplicated to 10 merged steps. Archive contributed: Ottawa Ankle Rules awareness in step 9, child fracture note folded into step 2, explicit shock monitoring language strengthened in step 10.
5 from canonical + 5 from archive. Deduplicated to 5 (all substantively identical across both guides).
6 from canonical + 5 from archive. Deduplicated to 6. Archive's 'Do not use tight compression' and 'Do not give food/drink' already present in canonical.
6 from canonical + 6 from archive. Deduplicated to 7. Archive contributed unique item: 'Child with significant limb pain after trauma — fractures in children can be subtle; get imaging'.
3 from canonical + 3 from archive. Deduplicated to 4. Archive contributed unique item about Ottawa Ankle Rules for deciding urgent care vs ER.

Both guides cite the same 3 sources (Red Cross Muscle/Bone/Joint, Red Cross Fractures, AHA/Red Cross 2024). All have real URLs. Kept 3 refs with canonical's whyUseful descriptions (slightly more detailed).
10 from canonical + 10 from archive. Deduplicated to 11. Archive added 'RICE' (already in canonical) and 'medical' (excluded as too generic).
4 from canonical + 3 from archive. Deduplicated to 5. Archive contributed unique item about deciding between emergency care and urgent care.

fieldDecisions.note: Archive was scenario layer. Cross-layer merge; canonical layer retained.
Archive was scenario_guide. Canonical type retained.

fieldDecisions.source: immobilize-sprain-or-fracture has 2 images (improvised-splint, ankle-wrap). Archive has none.

conflicts.field: stepByStepActions — step ordering (splint before cold pack vs interleaved)
conflicts.valueA: Steps 4-6: splint, then step 7: cold pack, then step 8: elevate
conflicts.valueB: Steps 4-5: splint, then step 6: cold pack, then step 7: elevate
conflicts.resolution: Same sequence, just different step numbering. Both agree: splint first, then cold, then elevate. No substantive conflict.

conflicts.valueA: No explicit step about arranging medical evaluation (covered implicitly in 'keep the person still until help arrives')
conflicts.valueB: Step 9: 'Arrange medical evaluation: urgent care or an emergency room depending on severity. Any suspected fracture or inability to bear weight needs imaging to rule out a break.'
conflicts.resolution: Added archive's step 9 content as a merged step — valuable decision-making guidance. Fits within 10-step cap.

archiveCandidateWarnings: sprains-and-suspected-fractures v2 has alternativeToGuideSlugs: ['immobilize-sprain-or-fracture'] — this points TO the canonical slug, so archiving is safe (no dangling references).
No other guides in the library have alternativeToGuideSlugs pointing to sprains-and-suspected-fractures — safe to archive.
sprains-and-suspected-fractures has a current_published_version_id set. The archive action should set review_status to 'archived' on this version after the merged version is approved.
